tasks.label: Give written notice to your landlord/agent per your tenancy agreement and local law; confirm the required notice length and any rules on when it must take effect
tasks.dueText: Check your agreement for the required notice period

tasks.label: Ask your landlord/agent for their checkout requriements and final inspection arrangements
tasks.dueText: Checkout process, key return method, cleaning expectations, garden expectations, final inspection date & time

tasks.label: Book removals or van hire / rental
Set up mail forwarding/redirection with your postal service
Book end-of-tenancy professional cleaning if required by agreement (keep receipt)
Book professional carpet cleaning if required by agreement (keep receipt)
Book professional upholstery/pet-odour cleaning if you had pets and your agreement requires it (keep the receipt)
Notify gas, electricity and water suppliers of your leaving date
Notify broadband provider: cancel, transfer, or take connectivity with you
Notify contents insurance: cancel, transfer, or update
Update any broadcasting, TV or media licence/fee that applies in your country
Notify the relevant authority for any local property/occupancy taxes or municipal charges of your leaving date and request a final bill
Give your landlord/agent a forwarding address for the deposit return and any final post
Cancel or return any resident/on-street parking permit(s) to the relevant authority or building management
Replace dead lightbulbs with the same type/wattage as the inventory
Mow the lawn, weed and trim hedges if the garden is your responsibility
Repair minor damage permitted by agreement (filler, touch-up paint matched to existing)
Deep clean oven, hob, extractor fan and grill
Defrost and clean the fridge and freezer (allow 24 hours)
Clean washing machine drum, seal, detergent drawer and filter
Clean dishwasher filter and run an empty hot cycle with cleaner
Descale showerhead, taps and kettle, and remove limescale from tiles
Bleach toilets including under the rim and behind the seat
Clean grout and silicone seals in bathrooms and kitchen if mouldy
Wipe skirting boards, door frames, light switches and sockets
Wipe inside all cupboards, wardrobes and drawers
Clean inside and outside of all windows and window tracks
Vacuum and mop every floor including under furniture and behind appliances
Remove all picture hooks and fill holes if the agreement requires
Empty and clean all bins; arrange large-item collection via your local waste service or a charity
Take date-stamped photos and/or video of every empty room, cupboard and appliance
Clear all belongings from every room, plus the loft, shed, garage and any outbuildings
tasks.dueText: Ensure to check up high on shelves and cupboards

tasks.label: Photograph final meter readings (gas, electricity, water and any separate heating/oil meter)
tasks.dueText: Ensure the meter serial numbers are visible

tasks.label: Return all keys and fobs: front, back, windows, mailbox, communal, bin store, parking, garage
Switch off power at the consumer unit / fuse board only if instructed
Conduct the check-out inventory inspection with your landlord/agent if possible; get it emailed or signed
Secure the property on your final exit (windows closed, doors locked, heating off, non-essential appliances off, lights off)
Submit final meter readings to suppliers the same day
Get written confirmation/receipt that all keys and fobs were returned, with the date and time
Request your deposit / security bond return in writing, referencing how it was held/protected
tasks.dueText: Confirm any timescale for response under your agreement or local law

tasks.label: If there is a deposit dispute, raise it via your local deposit scheme, tribunal, or small-claims process
Pay final utility bills and only then cancel direct debits
tasks.dueText: Cancelling outstanding direct debits first risks credit issues

tasks.label: Cancel the standing order / recurring payment for rent once the final payment has cleared
Confirm the local property/occupancy tax account is closed and the final bill settled
Update all banks, building societies, mortgage lenders and credit cards with your new address
Update tax-advantaged savings/investment accounts, brokerage and exchange accounts with your new address
Update life insurance, critical illness and income protection with your new address
Update solicitor, accountant and will/executor records with your new address
Update your driving licence address with the relevant authority
tasks.dueText: Some jurisdictions fine you for not updating it promptly

tasks.label: Update your vehicle registration / log book with the relevant authority
Update your vehicle insurance address
Update any disabled parking permit or badge with the relevant authority
Update workplace and personal pension providers with your new address
Notify your tax authority of your address change (income tax, social security/national insurance and any tax credits)
Update your business/company registration address (registered office and director's service address) if you run a business
Update any government benefits, student finance provider or social security bodies you deal with
Update vet records and pet microchip database if you have pets
Update default shipping addresses with frequently used online retailers and payment services
Update gym and other subscription services (e.g. mobile provider) with your new address
Update your voter registration at your new address, if applicable
Check if any post is still arriving for you at the old address after a few months
Keep tenancy agreement, inventory, photos and correspondence for at least the limitation period for disputes in your jurisdiction (often several years)
Reject deductions for fair wear and tear
tasks.dueText: Landlord must prove damage beyond normal use

tasks.label: Reject 'betterment' deductions
tasks.dueText: Landlord cannot charge new-for-old on used items — replacement must be like-for-like

tasks.label: Reference the move-in inventory and your amendments for any disputes
tasks.dueText: Note if damage was already present and documented, or if their provided inventory has poor-quality images that do not prove the damage did not already exist
